title: Component Governance Rules
description: Schema for a component's normative governance rules — the citable, component-tier rules compiled into the design system's governance index. Descriptive component data (API, variants, a11y) lives in the component-spec skill's component-spec.schema.json.

$schema.description: Path to this JSON Schema for IDE validation and autocomplete.

name.description: PascalCase component name (e.g. 'Button', 'Card'). Must match the spec's component.name.

scope.description: The component's scope code from the governance scope registry (e.g. 'BTN'). Every rule id must be prefixed with it.

rules.description: The component-tier governance rules. Each is citable by its authored, stable id.

kind.description: anti-pattern: a usage to avoid. parent-constraint: variants restricted within a named parent context.

scenario.description: anti-pattern only — what NOT to do.

alternative.description: anti-pattern only — what to do instead.

context.description: parent-constraint only — the parent context this constraint applies in (e.g. 'form-email-collection').

description.description: parent-constraint only — what this context is.

forbidden.description: parent-constraint only — variants that must not be used in this context.

recommended.description: parent-constraint only — variants to prefer instead.

status.description: Mark a rule repealed: it stays in the authored YAML (its citation is retired, never reused) but is dropped from the generated governance index.

ruleId.description: Authored, stable component-tier rule citation, prefixed with the component's scope code (e.g. 'BTN-2'). Never reused once retired.

severity.description: How hard the rule pushes back. Defaults: parent-constraint → warning, anti-pattern → info. Set to override.
